llm_pattern: |I| spans a very wide range and increases strongly with increasing |V| on both negative and positive sides. The smallest |I| values occur near low |V|, and the largest |I| values occur near |V|≈6 V. The positive and negative branches are broadly similar in magnitude, with small branch-to-branch differences and local non-monotonic fluctuations at higher |V|.

llm_keywords.evidence: |I| ranges from 8.90e-103 A at V=-0.1 V to 2.399e-05 A at V=-6 V, a span of about 97.43 decades.
The smallest listed |I| values occur near low |V|: 8.90e-103 A at -0.1 V and 6.90e-103 A at +0.1 V.

On the positive side, |I| rises from 7.83e-15 A at 1.0 V to 1.91e-09 A at 2.0 V and to 2.25e-05 A at 6.0 V; on the negative side, |I| rises from 6.62e-15 A at -1.0 V to 1.78e-09 A at -2.0 V and to 2.40e-05 A at -6.0 V.
Matched-voltage magnitudes are close on both sides, for example |I| is 1.78e-09 A at -2.0 V versus 1.91e-09 A at +2.0 V, and 7.48e-06 A at -5.0 V versus 9.23e-06 A at +5.0 V.
At higher |V| there are local reversals instead of strict stepwise monotonic change, such as +5.45 V: 1.86e-05 A, +5.50 V: 1.80e-05 A, +5.55 V: 1.30e-05 A, then +5.80 V: 2.24e-05 A.
